name: send_message_tool
summary: Deliver a message to a named peer agent in the same run-tree.
The recipient picks the message up automatically on its next step — there is no inbox to poll. Returns a one-line confirmation naming the recipient.
constraints: The sender's identity is added by the framework — the recipient sees your agent name as the sender; you do not pass it.
Cross-run delivery is not supported. The recipient must be running (or paused with `keep_alive`) under the same orchestrator.
Sending to a name that is not currently running leaves the message in the inbox indefinitely; it is delivered if and when an agent with that name later starts.
anti_patterns: To spawn a new agent — use `agent_tool`.
To return a result to your caller — just finish your step normally; the caller awaits your output.
To broadcast — there is no fan-out; call `send_message_tool` once per recipient.
cautions: IMPORTANT: you cannot send a message to yourself. The tool rejects `to == <your name>`.
output: Plain text. `delivered to <name>` on success, or an error message (sending to self, missing runtime context, missing inbox).

to.description: Name of the recipient agent. Must match a peer in the same run-tree.

message.description: Body of the message. Plain text; appears as a user-role message in the recipient's next step.

summary.description: Optional 5-10 word preview shown in the recipient's header line. Useful when the body is long.
